conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, I'm so tired of the same pop singers on every playlist. Do you know any bands that actually sound different?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, definitely. You should check out this jazz-folk group called The Quiet Hours. They're nothing like what's on the radio.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Jazz-folk? I've never even heard of that mix.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's mostly acoustic guitar with a little saxophone. Really relaxing. They've got a new album that just dropped last week.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That sounds perfect for studying. Where can I listen to them?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: They're on every streaming app. Just search the band name and start with the album — the first track is the best one.

questions.stem: What kind of music does the man recommend?
questions.explanation: 正确答案A：男生明确推荐了一个叫The Quiet Hours的jazz-folk（爵士民谣）乐队。B选项错误，对话从未提到摇滚乐。C选项错误，女生恰恰是厌倦了流行歌手，男生推荐的不是流行歌手。D选项错误，电台播放列表正是女生想要避开的东西，而非推荐内容。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Buy a physical copy of the album at a store
options.B: Ask the man to make her a playlist
options.C: Search for the band on a streaming app
options.D: Go to one of the band's live concerts

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：男生说乐队在所有流媒体应用上都有，让女生搜索乐队名字，女生对此很感兴趣，所以她接下来最可能去流媒体上搜索。A选项过度推断，对话只提到流媒体，没人说要买实体唱片。B选项错误，女生并没有请男生帮她做播放列表。D选项过度推断，对话完全没有提到现场演唱会。

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So I finally saw that mystery movie everyone's been talking about last night.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, the one with the detective? Was it as good as people say?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: The twists were incredible — I genuinely didn't see the ending coming. But honestly, it was way too long. Almost three hours.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Three hours? Yikes. I can barely sit still for two.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right? The middle really dragged. They could've cut a whole subplot and nothing would change.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm. Maybe I'll just wait and stream it at home, so I can pause when I get restless.

questions.stem: What does the woman say about the movie?

options.B: It had great twists but was too long
options.C: The ending was easy to predict
options.D: The middle section was the best part

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：女生说反转非常精彩（twists were incredible），但电影太长了（way too long）。A选项错误，她从未评价演技。C选项错误，她说自己根本没猜到结局（didn't see the ending coming），与"容易预测"相反。D选项错误，她说中段拖沓（really dragged），并非最精彩的部分。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do?

options.A: Go see the movie in the theater this weekend
options.C: Ask the woman to lend him the DVD
options.D: Watch the movie at home so he can pause it

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：男生说自己坐不住，打算在家用流媒体看，这样累了能暂停（stream it at home, so I can pause）。A选项与他的话相反，他明确说要在家看而非去影院。B选项错误，他没有说完全不看，只是改换在家看。C选项错误，对话从未提到DVD或向女生借。

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I really want to do something this semester besides class, but I can't decide. Should I join the debate club or the intramural soccer team?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm. Well, what are you actually looking to get out of it?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Mostly to meet people and have a regular reason to leave my room. The debate club meets twice a week, which feels like a lot of prep.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, debate is a big time commitment. Soccer's more casual — you just show up and play, and everyone goes for food after.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That social part really appeals to me. And I don't have to be good at soccer, right?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not at all. It's all just for fun. I think you'd love it.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to improve the woman's soccer skills
options.B: Whether the debate club is too competitive
options.C: Which campus activity the woman should join
options.D: How to manage a heavy course schedule

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：整段对话围绕女生该参加辩论俱乐部还是校内足球队展开，这是核心话题。A选项太窄，提升球技只是顺带提到，不是主题。B选项太窄，辩论的时间投入只是讨论的一个细节。D选项错误，对话谈的是课外活动，而非如何安排繁重课程。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.B: Sign up for the debate club instead
options.D: Try out for the varsity soccer squad

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：女生被足球队的社交氛围吸引，且得知不必踢得好，男生也鼓励她，所以她最可能加入校内足球队。B选项与对话趋势相反，她觉得辩论准备太花时间。C选项错误，她已明显倾向某一选择，没有表示要拖延。D选项过度推断，她想要的是休闲娱乐，而非加入校队竞技。

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Are we still on for the hiking trip this Saturday? I've been looking forward to it all week.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Absolutely. The forecast says clear skies, so it should be perfect.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Great. What time should we leave? The trailhead is about an hour's drive.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let's leave by seven. That way we beat the crowds and the heat. Oh, and don't forget to bring lots of water.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good call. Should I pack lunch for both of us, or are we stopping somewhere?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: There's no place to eat on the trail, so yeah — pack something. I'll bring snacks and the first-aid kit.

questions.stem: What does the man tell the woman to bring?

options.C: A map of the hiking route

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：男生明确提醒女生别忘了带很多水（don't forget to bring lots of water）。A选项错误，急救包是男生自己负责带的。B选项错误，零食也是男生说他来带。C选项错误，对话完全没有提到地图。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the woman's role in the trip?

options.A: She will drive everyone to the trailhead
options.B: She will be responsible for bringing lunch
options.C: She plans to cancel if the weather changes
options.D: She has hiked this particular trail before

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：女生主动提出由她为两人准备午餐，男生说路上没地方吃饭，让她带上，所以午餐归她负责。A选项过度推断，对话没说谁开车，只说要开一小时。C选项错误，天气预报是晴天，没人提到取消。D选项过度推断，对话没有任何迹象表明她以前走过这条路。

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I bought this sweater last week, but it turned out to be too small. I'd like to return it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, I can help with that. Do you have the receipt with you?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I do, right here. Although — I actually still love the design. Is the same one available in a medium?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me check the system. Yes, we've got mediums in stock, but only in the navy and the gray, not the original burgundy.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, I really wanted the burgundy. Could you order it in my size?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I can place a special order. It'd take about a week to arrive, and we'd hold it here for you to pick up.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, let me think — a week isn't too bad.

questions.stem: Why does the woman come to the store?

options.A: To return a sweater that does not fit
options.B: To complain about a defective sweater
options.C: To ask for a refund on a damaged receipt
options.D: To buy a sweater as a gift for a friend

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：女生开门见山说毛衣太小想退货，这是她来店的主要原因。B选项错误，毛衣没有质量问题，只是尺码不合。C选项错误，她带着完好的收据，没有提到收据损坏。D选项错误，毛衣是她自己买的，对话没有提到送礼。

questions.stem: What is the woman most likely to do?

options.A: Take the gray sweater in a medium right away
options.B: Get a full refund and leave the store
options.C: Place a special order for the burgundy sweater
options.D: Return next week to look for other colors

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生坚持要酒红色，店员提出可以特别订货一周到货，女生回应"一周不算太糟"，倾向于接受订货。A选项被她否决，她明确说想要酒红色而非灰色。B选项错误，她仍然喜欢这个款式，并未表示只想退款走人。D选项错误，她不是过一周再来挑别的颜色，而是为酒红色下特别订单等取货。
